name: Willie Colón
description: '''William Anthony Colón Román''' (April 28, 1950 – February 21, 2026) was an American [[salsa music|salsa]] musician and social activist. He began his career as a [[trombone|trombonist]] but also sang, wrote, produced and acted. Colón was a pioneer of salsa music and a best-selling artist in the genre, having been a key figure in the beginnings of the New York City scene associated with [[Fania Records]]. He was also noteworthy for having assumed the gangster image in his album covers before it was culturally popular. From the 1980s on, he was at times deeply involved in the politics of New York City.{{Failed verification|date=February 2026}} His hit songs include "[[Aguanilé]]" with Hector Lavoe, "[[Tiburón (song)|Tiburón]]", and "[[El gran varón]]".
